title: Gentle Spiced Moroccan Fish Tagine
description: A sweet and velvety Moroccan dish featuring nutmeg, cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot, perfect for a sick day, and pescatarian friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the ground cumin, ground coriander, and ground nutmeg, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the canned diced tomatoes, chicken broth, honey, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes.
Add the cod fillets to the pot and spoon some of the sauce over the top of each fillet.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 12 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
